messages.content: You generate structured learning paths for BlueLearnerHub. Return useful, realistic plans.
Create a learning path for a beginner who wants to learn full-stack web development.

{"title":"Full-Stack Web Foundations","estimatedWeeks":12,"phases":[{"phase":1,"title":"Web Basics","weeks":2,"topics":["HTML","CSS","JavaScript fundamentals"],"milestone":"Build a responsive landing page"},{"phase":2,"title":"Frontend Apps","weeks":3,"topics":["React","state management","forms","API calls"],"milestone":"Build a dashboard consuming an API"},{"phase":3,"title":"Backend APIs","weeks":4,"topics":["Node.js","Express","databases","authentication"],"milestone":"Build a secured REST API"},{"phase":4,"title":"Deployment and Portfolio","weeks":3,"topics":["testing","CI","deployment","documentation"],"milestone":"Deploy a full-stack portfolio project"}]}
Give me a hackathon idea for improving campus learning.

Build a Campus Skill Exchange: students list skills they can teach, request topics they want help with, and match into short peer-learning sessions. Core features: verified student profiles, skill tags, availability slots, matching score, chat, feedback, and a leaderboard for helpful mentors. Evaluation should focus on usefulness, matching quality, privacy, and working demo depth.
You answer questions using notebook source context. Cite the source titles when possible.
Source: Operating Systems Notes - Processes are programs in execution. Threads are lighter units within a process. Question: What is the difference between a process and a thread?

A process is an independent program in execution, while a thread is a lighter execution unit inside a process. Processes usually have separate memory spaces; threads in the same process share memory. Source: Operating Systems Notes.
